topic: festival lineup opinions — who to see

turns.content: ok so i need opinions on something. if you had to choose between seeing two artists at a festival that are playing at the exact same time and you physically cannot split yourself in half... how do you decide

Tough one. I'd usually go with: which one would you regret missing more? Not which one you "should" see, but which one you'd be gutted about if your friends came back saying it was incredible.

Who are the two artists?
ok so the lineup just dropped for this festival im going to and japanese breakfast and king gizzard are playing at the same time on the saturday and i am in genuine agony about it

Oh that IS a brutal clash. They're completely different experiences too, which makes it harder — Japanese Breakfast is that gorgeous, emotionally devastating indie set and King Gizzard is pure chaotic energy.

Have you seen either of them live before?

ive seen king gizz twice actually and theyre incredible live every time, like they just go SO hard. but i have never seen japanese breakfast and ive been obsessed with jubilee for literally years. michelle zauner is one of my favourite humans. did you know she wrote a whole memoir about her mum? crying in h mart? genuinely devastating book

Crying in H Mart is such a beautiful book — the way she writes about food and grief and cultural identity is really powerful.

Honestly, based on what you just said, I think you already know the answer. You've seen King Gizz twice and loved them — you know what that experience is. Japanese Breakfast is the unknown, you've been wanting to see her for years, and there's an emotional connection with her work beyond just the music. That feels like the one you'd regret missing.

ugh youre right. i know youre right. the thing is king gizz does a different setlist every time and theyre touring a new album so it would be a completely different show... but yeah. yeah. japanese breakfast. i think i need to see her play be sweet live or i will literally never recover

Be Sweet live would be absolutely electric in a festival setting. And honestly, King Gizz tours so frequently in Australia that you'll get another chance — they seem to come through every year or two. Japanese Breakfast tours less often, so the scarcity factor is on her side too.

What else on the lineup are you keen for?

ok so sunday is heaps better cos theres no clashes for me. genesis owusu is playing and he is a MUST see. also amyl and the sniffers which is going to be absolutely feral in the best way. and theres this smaller act called surprise chef that does like instrumental soul funk stuff and theyre from melbourne so i feel obligated to support lol

That's a stacked Sunday. Genesis Owusu is phenomenal live — his stage presence is unreal. And Amyl and the Sniffers at a festival is going to be pure chaos, you'll come out of that one absolutely wrecked.

Surprise Chef is a great pick too — their stuff has that vintage soundtrack quality that works so well in a live setting. Supporting local acts is always worth it, especially when they're actually good.

Sounds like Sunday is the stronger day for you overall.

it really is. saturday is more about the vibes and wandering around and getting food and seeing japanese breakfast as the main event. sunday is go mode from the arvo onwards. i reckon ill be completely dead by monday but thats a future lily problem

That's a solid festival strategy honestly — one chill exploration day and one intense go-hard day. Your Monday self will hate your Sunday self but that's the unwritten contract of every festival.

Are you going with a group or solo?

going with a few mates from uni which is good cos it means someone can hold my spot if i need to go get food during a set. also we can split up for clashes and just report back on what we saw. lowkey the post-set debrief is half the fun of going with people honestly

The post-set debrief is genuinely underrated — comparing notes on who was good, who was disappointing, who had the surprise set of the day. That's festival culture at its best.

Have you figured out your logistics yet? Like camping or day passes?

day passes cos no way am i sleeping in a tent in february heat lol. im not built for festival camping. id rather just drive home and sleep in an actual bed like a normal person and come back the next day. ok im actually getting so keen now i need to stop looking at the lineup and go to bed its 1am

Day passes are the smart move — festival camping in Australian summer heat is genuinely brutal. Nothing ruins a Sunday lineup like zero sleep and heatstroke from a tent that turns into an oven at 6am.

Get some sleep! The lineup will still be there tomorrow.
